reasoning:
Aronian's ranking on April 13 (via the April 1 list on Wikipedia) is 14, with a rating of 2747. The resolution threshold is rank <= 14. 
- April 20 falls in the same FIDE month, so the list will be unchanged (100% chance, practically). 
- By May 13 (the May 1 list), Aronian is unlikely to have played classical chess since he is scheduled for the Freestyle event in mid-April. Looking at current ratings, there are three active players just 1-2 points ahead of him (Aravindh 2749, So 2748, Mamedyarov 2748) and the nearest active challengers are roughly 8-11 points behind (Duda 2739, Fedoseev 2739, Giri 2738, Niemann 2736, as Anand who is -4 is inactive). Because of this asymmetrical buffer, normal random rating variance over a single month is more likely to drop one of the players ahead of him down behind him, moving him up in rank, than for an active player below to gain enough (8+ points) to overtake him. Thus, his probability of staying <= 14 in May is highly favorable.
- By July and October, the time horizon allows more rating volatility. For a 42-year-old player on a multi-year general downward drift, the base rate expectation over 6 months should begin to dip below 50%. Thus, we increase uncertainty for July (64%) and drop slightly below the midline for October (47%).
